definitions: The condition of being relentless.

definitions: The property or state of being relevant or pertinent.

definitions: A relevant thing. [from 19th c.]

definitions: (slang, by extension) Famous, popular, or noteworthy in a contemporary context.

definitions: In a relevant way.
The quality of being reliable, dependable, or trustworthy.
(engineering) measurable time of work before failure

(education) the ability to measure the same thing consistently (of a measurement indicating the degree to which the measure is consistent); that is, repeated measurements would give the same result (See also validity).
examples: Above all, rail needs to be boring. By that, I mean that it must run its timetable reliably, day-in and day-out. Punctuality and reliability remain the bedrock of a successful railway.

definitions: Something or someone reliable or dependable
examples: the old reliables

definitions: (signal processing, of a communication protocol) Such that either a sent packet will reach its destination, even if it requires retransmission, or the sender will be told that it didn't.

definitions: The property of being reliable, reliability.

examples: Genuine cowards follow the wicked and cannot reliably sustain any virtue.

definitions: The condition of being reliant or dependent.

definitions: Having reliance on somebody or something.

definitions: (cosmology) A particle or entity that has existed since the Big Bang.

definitions: The feeling associated with the removal of stress or discomfort.

definitions: Belief in a spiritual or metaphysical reality (often including at least one deity), accompanied by practices or rituals pertaining to the belief.
